name: Calabria (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic azure quarters of the regional shield

description: A white field charged at the centre with the regional coat of arms of Calabria: a quartered shield bearing the historic emblems of the region's provinces, ensigned with a mural crown and surrounded by a green wreath.
history: Adopted alongside the Regional Statute (Regional Law n. 25 of 19 October 2004). The quartered shield assembles emblems traditionally associated with Calabria's historic provinces, anchoring the modern administrative region to its constituent lands.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the regional coat of arms of Calabria, a quartered shield uniting historic emblems of the region's component lands.

features.role: regional coat of arms of Calabria (a quartered shield bearing the historic arms of the four provinces, surmounted by a mural crown and surrounded by a wreath)
features.symbolism: The four quarters bring together emblems associated with the historic provinces of Cosenza, Catanzaro, Reggio Calabria and Crotone, recalling Calabria's classical heritage as the toe of the Italian peninsula.
